title: Though I Thy Mithridates Were
content:
Though I thy Mithridates were,
    Framed to defy the poison-dart,
    Yet must thou fold me unaware
    To know the rapture of thy heart,
    And I but render and confess
    The malice of thy tenderness.

    For elegant and antique phrase,
    Dearest, my lips wax all too wise;
    Nor have I known a love whose praise
    Our piping poets solemnize,
    Neither a love where may not be
    Ever so little falsity.
meaning:
"Though I Thy Mithridates Were" is a quintessential example of James Joyce's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
